discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape led by semis (SMH +2.6%, AVGO +5%, TSM +5.3%) on SK Hynix record profit and AI memory demand, while defense (ITA -1.8%) lags on Iran ceasefire hopes despite tanker interception headlines. Biggest mid-cap dislocation is CALX -14% on heavy volume; MANH strength and BBY weakness warrant attention.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.62% and XLK +2.2% driven by SK Hynix record Q1, AVGO +5%, TSM +5.3%
ITA -1.77% diverges from broad rally — Iran ceasefire narrative plus Navy Secretary departure
XLF slightly red and KRE -0.55% despite risk-on tape — watch for rotation, not capitulation
VIX 18.92, modestly lower — no stress signal

discoveries.name: Calix, Inc
discoveries.mechanism: Likely earnings reaction — magnitude and 4x volume suggest guidance miss or specific disappointment, but no news item in the feed confirms. Sympathy drag on EXTR (-5.8%) points to shared communication-equipment/broadband spending concern.
discoveries.catalyst: Probable Q1 earnings/guidance (unconfirmed in provided news)
research_pointers: Pull CALX Q1 print and forward guidance — was it a revenue guide cut or margin issue?
Check BEAD/broadband subsidy commentary — CALX is levered to rural broadband spend
Compare to peers ADTN, CIEN, EXTR for read-through on carrier/enterprise capex
Check whether insider selling or share offering accompanied the print
discoveries.what_would_falsify: A clean beat-and-raise would falsify a fundamental read and suggest technical/positioning flush (buying opportunity). Conversely, a guide cut citing systemic demand weakness validates the drop and implicates EXTR further.

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks
discoveries.mechanism: Sympathy selling from CALX print on a strongly risk-on day for tech (XLK +2.2%) — EXTR trading opposite its sector suggests peer read-across on networking/enterprise demand. If CALX miss is company-specific (rural broadband), EXTR reaction is likely overdone.
discoveries.catalyst: CALX earnings-driven sympathy in communication equipment
research_pointers: Determine if CALX weakness is end-market-specific (broadband/BEAD) vs. broad networking
Check EXTR's own earnings calendar — pre-announcement risk?
Review CSCO, ANET, JNPR tape for sympathy confirmation or divergence
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If ANET/CSCO are up with the tape, EXTR's drop is isolated sympathy and likely a fade. If they're also red, there's a broader enterprise networking concern.

discoveries.name: Best Buy
discoveries.mechanism: BBY down 4.6% on a risk-on tape with XLY flat is a notable divergence; GME +4.9% in same industry classification. Possible catalysts: tariff refund/Supreme Court Trump post raising tariff uncertainty, or specific BBY channel check / downgrade. Volume only 1.2x, so not a blowout news event.
discoveries.catalyst: No specific news in feed — possible analyst action or tariff-related uncertainty from Trump SCOTUS post
research_pointers: Scan for sell-side downgrades or channel checks on consumer electronics
Look at AAPL strength (+2.6%) vs. BBY weakness — potential read-through on direct-to-consumer shift
Check if any tariff-refund ruling commentary implicates BBY-specific inventory/margin
discoveries.what_would_falsify: An identified downgrade or guide-lower from a peer (DKS, WSM) would classify as rational; absence would suggest overdone.

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates
discoveries.mechanism: Strong move on 2x volume with 5-day +10.9%, likely earnings-driven (supply chain software). In context of NOW dropping 14% on Iran-war subscription hit, MANH strength could signal MANH avoided that headwind — differentiated read for supply chain SaaS.
discoveries.catalyst: Probable Q1 earnings beat (unconfirmed in feed)
research_pointers: Confirm MANH earnings date and results
Compare to NOW commentary — did MANH call out any Iran/geopolitical exposure?
Read-through to SPS Commerce, Descartes (DSGX), Kinaxis
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If move is pre-earnings momentum without print, risk of fade into the release. If print confirmed with raised guide, move may still have room.

discoveries.name: Repligen
discoveries.mechanism: Down 6% against flat-to-positive XBI and XLV; volume only modestly elevated suggests company-specific news (guide or earnings) rather than sector flow.
research_pointers: Check for RGEN earnings or pre-announcement
Review bioprocessing peers (DHR, Sartorius, West Pharma) for sympathy or divergence
Scan for FDA/regulatory or large-customer order commentary
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Peer weakness in bioprocessing would suggest sector theme; isolated move points to company-specific issue.

catalyst_chains.primary_event: SK Hynix record Q1 profit on AI memory demand

secondary_opportunities.relationship: supplier (semicap equipment beneficiary of memory capex)

catalyst_chains.primary_event: ServiceNow -14% citing Iran war subscription impact

secondary_opportunities.relationship: enterprise SaaS peer showing divergence — demonstrates not all SaaS is impaired

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Iran ceasefire optimism despite tanker interception reports; Navy Secretary departs

secondary_opportunities.relationship: defense ETF drag — watch for individual defense mid-caps (HII, LHX, KTOS) that may be over-sold if ceasefire fragility returns

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump rails against SCOTUS tariff ruling requiring $159B in tariff refunds and criticizes court on birthright citizenship case
trump_signals.note: The tariff refund ruling is a past event but Trump's reaction signals continued tariff-policy volatility. Import-heavy retailers (BBY mentioned above) could see both tailwinds (refund cash) and headwinds (new tariff threats). BBY's -4.6% today may partly reflect this uncertainty rather than refund optimism. Low conviction on directionality — worth monitoring for follow-up executive actions.

trump_signals.post_summary: Reported Trump readiness to reclassify cannabis (prior news item)
trump_signals.note: Cannabis reclassification is a genuine potential catalyst for the sector — not in the mid-cap movers list but worth flagging as a chain. US MSOs (GTBIF, CURLF, TCNNF) typically move more than Canadian LPs on US policy. Verify executive order status before acting.

discovery.no_signals_note: Biggest issue: CALX and MANH moves strongly suggest earnings prints but no confirming news item was provided, so classifications default to UNCLEAR. Michael should verify earnings releases before drawing conclusions.

ai_announcements.headline: Google updates Workspace with AI 'office intern', Chrome auto-browse, Gmail AI Overviews, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
ai_announcements.summary: Google Cloud Next 2026 unveiled a broad enterprise AI push: Workspace Intelligence automating office tasks, Chrome enterprise agent, Gmail AI summaries, and a new agent-building platform for IT.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Manhattan Associates' supply chain/WMS software is specialized vertical SaaS with deep ERP integration; generic office AI agents don't threaten it. Move is likely earnings-driven, not Google-driven.
research_pointers: Check if MANH reported earnings 4/22 (likely catalyst, not AI news)
Confirm no direct Google Maps/logistics AI product competes with MANH Active Omni

ai_announcements.headline: Google Cloud launches two new TPU AI chips to compete with Nvidia
ai_announcements.summary: New TPUs positioned as faster and cheaper than prior generation; Google still uses Nvidia in cloud.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco makes semicap equipment (lithography, deposition) used in advanced node chip manufacturing including AI accelerators. More TPU volume = more wafer starts = tailwind regardless of Nvidia vs TPU winner.
research_pointers: VECO exposure to TSMC/Samsung advanced packaging for TPUs
5-day +18% suggests broader semicap rally, not just today

ai_announcements.headline: General AI enterprise platform rollouts (Google, OpenAI/Infosys)
ai_announcements.summary: Broad enterprise AI automation announcements affecting networking/infrastructure vendor sentiment.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Calix sells broadband access platforms to rural telcos/ISPs. Zero direct overlap with Google Workspace AI. -14% on 4x volume is almost certainly an earnings/guidance event, not AI-driven.
research_pointers: Check CALX earnings report 4/22 after close - likely the real driver
Not an AI sympathy trade; do not conflate

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Extreme Networks sells enterprise networking hardware. AI buildouts typically INCREASE networking spend. Move likely earnings-related or sympathy to CALX weakness in networking/comm equipment.
research_pointers: Check EXTR earnings calendar
Sympathy selloff with CALX possible - potential OVERDONE if no company-specific news

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Best Buy could benefit from AI PC refresh cycle. Move likely tariff/consumer-cyclical driven, not AI news.
research_pointers: Check consumer discretionary sector move today
Not AI-related

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most flagged stock moves (CALX -14%, EXTR, RGEN, MANH, GME) appear driven by earnings or non-AI catalysts rather than today's AI announcements. No high-conviction AI-driven mispricing identified. Google Cloud Next is incrementally bullish for semicap (VECO rational) and modestly competitive pressure on Microsoft 365 / Salesforce / UiPath — but those names weren't in the movers list.
